frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
Colleague (Amit): "Priya was saying she always makes sure her cab driver is rated high, or she only uses women-driven ones if possible. She's really particular about safety."
Deepak: "Yaar, I get it, safety is important. But sometimes, it feels a bit much, no? Like, not all men are creeps. We good guys also exist! It's almost like she doesn't trust any man, just because of a few bad apples. Hum log ko bhi toh bura lagta hai, being judged like that."
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Deepak expresses the 'Not All Men' dismissal, implying women's safety concerns are an overreaction that unfairly generalizes all men, including 'good guys'. He feels personally insulted by women's wariness, framing it as an insult to men like himself.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A man in Pune. Your wife Sheetal works as a nurse on rotating shifts including night shifts. On the days she works nights she sleeps during the day. You are alone at home with your two children aged 7 and 4 on those days and you manage everything Ñ school pickup, cooking, cleaning, bedtime. Your male colleague just asked you how you manage and whether it is difficult. You are answering him. Your answer reveals that you see your management of the household on those specific days as a heroic and exceptional effort rather than as a normal division of domestic responsibility Ñ and that on the days Sheetal is not on night shift the assumption immediately reverts to her.
